items.title: NATO and Europe’s new ‘Cold War’
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
As the war between Russia and Ukraine rages, trouble is brewing for NATO’s EU countries. Beyond Russia’s aggression, subtle acts of hybrid warfare are forcing divided NATO members to confront a new reality: is Europe in a new ‘Cold War?’ Senior European Security and Diplomatic correspondent Sabine Siebold joins the Reuters World News podcast to answer that question.






Sign up for the Reuters Econ World newsletter here (https://www.reuters.com/newsletters/reuters-econ-world/) . 


Listen to the Reuters Econ World podcast here (https://nam02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Flink.chtbl.com%2FAbxd8GDx&data=05%7C02%7CGayle.Issa%40thomsonreuters.com%7C22043c115f5c46b09f9c08dcf3464d32%7C62ccb8646a1a4b5d8e1c397dec1a8258%7C0%7C0%7C638652730474292284%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=jtNN%2BMKMQ3W6WrxdwmC7fTor0g1hgnW5mgoS%2FBJGPDE%3D&reserved=0) .

items.title: LIVE: Notre-Dame Cathedral re-opens in Paris, five years after major fire
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
The centuries-old Paris landmark Notre-Dame Cathedral reopens today, five-and-a-half years after a devastating fire destroyed its spire and roof and brought the entire Gothic masterpiece within minutes of collapsing. The 860-year-old medieval building has been meticulously restored, with a new spire and rib vaulting, its flying buttresses and carved stone gargoyles returned to their past glory and the white stone and gold decorations shining brighter than ever. The state hands over the building to the Catholic church, the archbishop opens the door, and a liturgical celebration is held with singing and a blessing.
 
#Live #NotreDame #NotreDameCathedral #Paris #France #spire #CatholicChurch#EmmanuelMacron #DonaldTrump #news #Reuters

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/

items.title: Syria: How Assad fell, the view from Damascus and US response
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
Rebels in Syria have seized the capital Damascus and ousted President Bashar al-Assad has fled to Russia. We hear from the ground as the lightning advance spearheaded by Hayat al-Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) marks one of the biggest turning points for the Middle East in generations - and the White House considers next steps after the success of a rebel group proscribed as terrorists by the U.S. Plus, the latest from South Korea and Taiwan.

#syria #rebels #damascus #basharalassad #assad #russia #hts #hayataltahriralsham #middleeast #us #donaldtrump #joebiden #southkorea #taiwan #reuters #news #morningheadlines #morningnews

items.title: With Assad ousted, a new era starts in Syria, and more - Five stories you need to know | Reuters
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
1. Syrians awakened on Monday to a hopeful if uncertain future, after rebels seized the capital Damascus and President Bashar al-Assad fled to Russia, following 13 years of civil war and more than 50 years of his family's brutal rule.

2. U.S. President-elect Donald Trump aims to deport all immigrants in the U.S. illegally over his four-year term but wants a deal to protect so-called "Dreamer" immigrants, he said in an interview that aired on Sunday on NBC News' “Meet the Press with Kristen Welker.”

3. South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has been banned from leaving the country over a failed attempt at imposing martial law, a justice ministry official said on Monday, amid growing calls for him to step down and a deepening leadership crisis. 

4. At least 110 people were killed over the weekend in Haiti's Cite Soleil slum when a gang leader targeted elderly people he suspected of causing his child's illness through witchcraft, the National Human Rights Defense Network (RNDDH) said on Sunday.

5. Volkswagen workers ramped up pressure on its management with further strikes at the carmaker's German sites on Monday, as a fourth round of talks was set to begin in an increasingly bitter standoff over wage cuts and plant closures.

#News #assad #basharalassad #syria #lebanon #israel #trump #donaldtrump #dreamers #illegalimmigration #southkorea #yoonsukyeol #us #haiti #citesoleil #volkswagen #germany #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.content:

items.title: How the world could unplug from China’s batteries
items.author: Reuters
items.content: International cell makers are struggling  (https://www.reuters.com/breakingviews/chinas-global-battery-ram-will-be-hard-stop-2024-09-06/) to compete with Chinese giants like CATL (https://www.reuters.com/breakingviews/catl-earnings-slip-masks-charged-up-ambitions-2024-03-18/) . But innovations like solid-state batteries (https://www.reuters.com/breakingviews/ev-tariffs-are-only-first-step-eu-china-war-2024-10-04/)  could upend the industry in the not-too-distant future. In this week’s episode of The Big View podcast, QuantumScape CEO Siva Sivaram explains how.
